content:
✨ glances | Python
🎯 Primary Use Case: Real-time system performance monitoring and resource utilization analysis across multiple operating systems, accessible via terminal, web UI, or API.
✨ Key Features: • Cross-platform system monitoring (Linux, BSD, macOS, Windows) • Real-time monitoring of CPU, memory, disk, network, processes, etc. • Container monitoring (Docker, LXC)
📖 Summary: Glances is an open-source, cross-platform system monitoring tool written in Python. It provides real-time insights into CPU, memory, disk, network, processes, and container usage, with a user-friendly dashboard. It supports remote monitoring via a web interface, CLI, and RESTful API, and can export data to various formats or databases.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

✨ luna | Go
🎯 Primary Use Case: To provide a unified, customizable, and self-hosted interface for aggregating and viewing multiple digital calendars.
✨ Key Features: • Calendar aggregation from CalDav, iCal, Google Calendar • Single web application frontend • User management • Infinitely customizable themes and fonts • Built-in themes and easy installation of custom themes/fonts
📖 Summary: Luna is a self-hosted calendar aggregator and frontend application that consolidates calendars from various protocols like CalDav, iCal, and Google Calendar into a single, customizable web interface. It offers extensive theming options and user management, aiming to provide a unified and personalized calendar experience.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source

🌟 ai-file-sorter | C++
🎯 Primary Use Case: Intelligent, AI-powered organization and renaming of digital files on a desktop.
✨ Key Features: • Content-aware file organization and renaming • Supports local and remote LLMs (e.g., Llama 3B, Mistral 7B) • Preview-based workflows for user control • Automatic folder creation and file sorting • Privacy-first design with local processing options
📖 Summary: AI File Sorter is a cross-platform desktop application that leverages AI to intelligently organize and rename files like images, documents, and media. It offers a privacy-first approach by supporting local LLMs and providing preview-based workflows for user-controlled file management.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

🌟 phantom | TypeScript
🎯 Primary Use Case: To provide a persistent, autonomous AI co-worker that can independently manage tasks, build tools, and evolve its capabilities within its own dedicated environment, freeing up user resources.
✨ Key Features: • Persistent memory and self-evolution • Dedicated computing environment (its own computer) • Autonomous software installation and infrastructure building
📖 Summary: Phantom is an AI co-worker designed to be persistent and self-evolving, acting as an independent agent with its own dedicated computing environment. It goes beyond typical chatbots by installing software, managing data, and building infrastructure autonomously, improving its capabilities over time.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

🔥 claw-code | Rust
🎯 Primary Use Case: To provide a robust and efficient command-line interface for developers to build, manage, and interact with AI agents using Rust.
✨ Key Features: • Rust implementation of `claw` CLI agent harness • Command-line interface for AI agent interaction • Build and run agent workflows • Session and authentication management • Parity checking for Rust ports
📖 Summary: Claw Code is a Rust implementation of the `claw` CLI agent harness, designed to provide a command-line interface for interacting with AI agents. It focuses on building and running these agents, with features for managing sessions, authentication, and checking system parity.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source
